title: Opendoor (OPEN) Q1 Earnings Preview: Wall Street Braces for Stock Swing
text: Opendoor Technologies will report Q1 results on May 7, 2026. The company's stock has declined 12% year-to-date and currently trades near $5.11. Analysts project EPS of -$0.09, an improvement from -$0.12 in Q1 2025, and revenue of approximately $666 million, down from $1.15 billion year-over-year. Expected volatility suggests an 8.77% stock movement following the announcement. Fourth quarter results revealed 46% sequential growth in home purchases, though revenue decreased 20% period-over-period. The company also saw properties entering contract status surge over 300%, indicating mixed operational signals. The consensus rating is Hold with an average price target of $6, suggesting 17% upside potential. However, analyst Eric Jackson from EMJ Capital maintains an aggressive $82 price projection, reflecting about 1,400% appreciation from current levels. Alliance Global Partners' Gaurav Mehta offers an $8 price target, citing potential profitability improvements and market expansion opportunities. Elevated mortgage costs and inflated property valuations continue pressuring the real estate sector and Opendoor's instant-buying model.
